input: Merge forge + ceos-agents into a unified pipeline plugin with /build command. Auto-detection of mode (code/analysis/strategy/content), unified 10-phase pipeline, shared core extraction, commands-to-skills migration, agent merges.

config_provenance.agents.phase_1: Increased from 5→7: scope=5 migration with 7 distinct research domains (plugin architecture, agent merging, pipeline engine, mode adapters, backward compat, skill migration, test strategy)
config_provenance.agents.phase_2: Increased from 5→7: matches research question count for 1:1 question→answer mapping
config_provenance.agents.phase_3: Kept at 3: conservative/innovative/skeptical triad is optimal for architectural decisions
config_provenance.review.max_rounds: Increased from 3→4: risk=5 + ambiguity=4 warrants extra review iteration to catch migration edge cases
config_provenance.approval_gates: Kept at [3,4,6]: all three gates critical for high-risk migration — brainstorm gate catches architectural flaws, spec gate catches missing backward compat, plan gate catches sequencing errors
config_provenance.skip_phases: No phases skipped: scope=5 migration requires full pipeline — research (understand both codebases), brainstorm (explore merge strategies), spec (formal contract), TDD (structural tests), plan (task graph), execute, verify
config_provenance.timeout.per_phase: Increased from 600s→900s: large scope means more files to read/write per phase
config_provenance.timeout.total_pipeline: Increased from 7200s→10800s (3h): cross-cutting migration affecting 152 files needs extended total budget
config_provenance.models: No changes from defaults: opus for critical design decisions (phases 0,3,4,6,8-reviewers), sonnet for research and execution (phases 1,2,5,7,8-correctness,9). Risk=5 does not warrant upgrading sonnet phases to opus because the review loops and approval gates provide sufficient quality assurance.
